title: tarenaya torticarpa
intro: Tarenaya torticarpa, also known as the Mexican poppy or Mexican treacle, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a sedative, antispasmodic, and expectorant. The health benefits of Tarenaya torticarpa include its potential to relieve symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other medicinal products.
